meta.note: Title-specific book restrictions in African states that criminalise homosexuality. Compiled from a deep-research report (2026-05), then each dossier re-sourced to a primary/reputable URL before inclusion. The criminalisation angle is the selection lens only; reasons below reflect the actual documented motive of each restriction. Malawi titles (No Bride Price; Chief the Honourable Minister) were dropped: no retraceable censorship-notice source. For Bread Alone and Notre ami le roi were dropped: Morocco bans already in DB (book ids 786, 6567).

entries.title: The Correct Line? Uganda under Museveni
entries.description_ban: A consignment (~500 copies) of this Museveni-critical book was impounded by customs at Entebbe airport in October 2010, shortly before its Kampala launch; the government cited unspecified 'security' grounds. Later released.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Customs impoundment of a regime-critical title; documented by the African Centre for Media Excellence and international press.

entries.title: Controlling Consent: Uganda's 2016 Elections
entries.description_ban: In March 2017 Uganda Revenue Authority customs seized a consignment of 668 copies, declaring them 'liable to forfeiture' under the East African Community Customs Management Act for alleged false declaration. The book is an anthology of essays on the contested 2016 election.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Formal customs seizure notice citing 668 copies; strongest primary-document dossier in this batch.

entries.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid
entries.description_ban: Banned from Tanzanian schools in February 2023 by Education Minister Adolf Mkenda as one of 16 titles deemed contrary to Tanzanian culture and morals, amid anti-LGBTQ rhetoric. Schools risk deregistration for keeping the books.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named in a ministerial school-ban announcement; widely reported. Note: a review reading the series found no LGBTQ content, so reason recorded as 'moral' per the official cultural-norms justification.

entries.title: Rodrick Rules
entries.description_ban: Banned from Tanzanian schools in February 2023 as one of 16 titles deemed contrary to Tanzanian culture and morals (Diary of a Wimpy Kid series).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named in the February 2023 Tanzanian ministerial school-ban list.

entries.title: The Getaway
entries.description_ban: Banned from Tanzanian schools in February 2023 as one of 16 titles deemed contrary to Tanzanian culture and morals (Diary of a Wimpy Kid series). The minister's list rendered this as 'The Gateway'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named in the February 2023 Tanzanian ministerial school-ban list (as 'The Gateway').

entries.title: Sex Education: A Guide to Life
entries.description_ban: Banned from Tanzanian schools in February 2023 as one of 16 titles deemed contrary to Tanzanian culture and morals. A non-fiction sex-education guide tied to the Netflix series 'Sex Education' (foreword by show creator Laurie Nunn).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Named in the February 2023 Tanzanian ministerial school-ban list.

entries.title: Nigeria and Its Criminal Justice System
entries.description_ban: In December 2024 High Courts in Abuja (FCT) and Oyo State granted interim injunctions restraining further publication, sale and distribution following a defamation suit by Afe Babalola SAN; security agencies were directed to seize copies and booksellers (incl. Glendora, Amazon) withdrew it.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered interim restraint with seizure directive and bookseller withdrawal; widely reported by Nigerian press.

entries.title: Lesbian Diaries
entries.description_ban: Withdrawn by Morocco's Ministry of Culture from the 2022 Rabat International Book Fair after a homophobic online campaign (#NoToHomosexuality). The ministry claimed the title was not on the approved list; the author disputed this. PEN America condemned the removal.
entries.inclusion_rationale: State withdrawal of an explicitly lesbian novel from a national book fair; documented by PEN America, Morocco World News and EuroMed Rights. Native (Arabic) title to be added on review.

entries.title: Black Sunlight
entries.description_ban: Banned by the Censorship Board on 7 August 1981 and gazetted as an 'undesirable publication' on 23 August 1981, on grounds of obscenity and blasphemy. The ban was overturned in 1982 after appeals led by Musaemura Zimunya.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Formal censorship-board ban and gazette notice; well documented in Zimbabwean literary scholarship and press.

entries.title: Democracy Works: Turning Politics to Africa's Advantage
entries.description_ban: In March 2019 Zimbabwean state security agents seized a DHL consignment of the book at Robert Mugabe International Airport, destined for its Harare launch; the copies were reportedly taken to the president's office, delaying the launch.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Customs/security seizure of a co-author's consignment before a national launch; reported by VOA and confirmed by the Brenthurst Foundation (co-author's institution).
